library_status.weekly_downloads: ~10M+ (11,043 dependents in registry; fastest Node.js logger)

candidates.epoch_check: library available before subsystem epoch (pino 2016; aegis-fabric epoch 2026-03)
candidates.verdict_reason: 48-line custom JSON logger reinvents a solved category; pino is a direct drop-in with MIT license and active maintenance.

candidates.epoch_check: library available before subsystem epoch (lru-cache 2011; aegis-fabric epoch 2026-03)
candidates.verdict_reason: Standard TTL+LRU cache with 990M weekly downloads; lru-cache v10+ has native TTL support matching current hand-roll exactly.

library_status.weekly_downloads: not confirmed precisely; top result in category

candidates.epoch_check: library available before subsystem epoch (rate-limiter-flexible 2018; aegis-fabric epoch 2026-03)
blockers: Hand-roll's consume(tenantId, maxPerHour, agentId?) takes quota PER CALL — lazy-inits a bucket sized to that tenant's quota. RateLimiterMemory takes {points, duration} at CONSTRUCTION.
Workaround = Map<quota, RateLimiterMemory> + async API (breaks 'callers don't move') + try/catch for reject-as-control-flow.
Zero existing test coverage on the 429 path — run-tests.ts doesn't exercise rate limiting; swap would ship unverified.
candidates.verdict_reason: Flipped GO → HOLD 2026-05-29 Session 1 after reading callsites. Per assemble-first 'no false symmetry': swap is *worse* (loses variable-per-tenant-quota model) + *later* (~45 LOC wrapper to model what's currently 65 LOC of hand-roll), not just *later*. Revisit when tenants converge on fixed quota tiers OR rate-limit tests establish parity baseline.
candidates.verdict_reason_at_audit: Token-bucket rate limiter per composite key is exactly rate-limiter-flexible's core use case; supports Redis-backed scaling if needed later.

library_status.weekly_downloads: high (part of langchain ecosystem; hundreds of thousands of installs weekly)

candidates.epoch_check: CAUTION — langchain-text-splitters first released March 2024 as standalone package; cognitive-sensor epoch is pre-2026, touched 2026-05-27. Library was available well before last modification date.
blockers: Requires Python >=3.10; confirm cognitive-sensor runtime version before swap
candidates.verdict_reason: Identical word-boundary overlap chunker duplicated in two files; langchain-text-splitters RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter replaces both; Python 3.10 requirement needs env confirm.

candidates.epoch_check: Same as agent_excavator — library available before 2026-05 last modification
blockers: Duplicate of above candidate — resolve both in one PR; Python >=3.10 check shared
candidates.verdict_reason: Exact duplicate of agent_excavator.py hand-roll; eliminating both in one swap removes two sources of divergence drift.

candidates.epoch_check: library available before subsystem epoch (FastAPI 2018; cognitive-sensor epoch pre-2026)
blockers: Background-thread sync model in TriageHandler must be refactored — manual threading vs asyncio workers; rated MEDIUM confidence-swap-safe in 2A audit
candidates.verdict_reason: Raw http.server is a solved-category reinvention; FastAPI migration is straightforward for 2 endpoints but requires async/threading boundary review.

candidates.epoch_check: library available before subsystem epoch (FastAPI 2018; uasc-executor epoch 2026-04)
blockers: HMAC auth middleware must be ported; 4 endpoints only — LOW complexity swap
candidates.verdict_reason: HIGH confidence-swap-safe per 2A audit; BaseHTTPRequestHandler with manual JSON is textbook solved category; FastAPI + dependency injection handles HMAC cleanly.

candidates.epoch_check: library available before subsystem epoch (APScheduler 2009; cortex epoch 2026-04)
blockers: APScheduler v4 is still pre-release and API-unstable — must use v3.x only
v3 AsyncIOScheduler has different coroutine lifecycle than current while-loop; stop-event cancellation pattern must be rewritten
2A audit rated this LOW confidence-swap-safe with MEDIUM moat_risk — defer until cortex scheduler is stable enough to touch
candidates.verdict_reason: Library exists and is maintained, but APScheduler v4 pre-release status + asyncio integration complexity makes this a non-trivial swap; revisit when APScheduler v4 reaches stable.

library_status.last_release_date: 2024-03 (last publish; ~2 years stale as of audit date)
library_status.maintained: MARGINAL — no release since March 2024; active issues accumulating

candidates.epoch_check: library available before subsystem epoch (telegraf 2016; crucix epoch 2026-04)
blockers: telegraf last published March 2024 — 14 months stale at audit date; Telegram Bot API moves fast and crucix uses polling
grammY (grammy on npm) is the actively maintained successor with 1.26M weekly downloads, last release 2025; recommend grammY over telegraf
candidates.verdict_reason: telegraf itself is marginally maintained; swap to grammY instead — same polling/middleware pattern, TypeScript-native, actively maintained with 2025 releases; re-evaluate as GO against grammY.

candidates.epoch_check: library available before subsystem epoch (discord.js 2015; crucix epoch 2026-04)
blockers: A6 audit notes discord.js is already listed as optional dep in package.json — if so this may already be wired
File not fully audited in A6 — confirm hand-roll exists before scheduling swap
candidates.verdict_reason: discord.js is already in optional deps; need to confirm the actual implementation before calling this a hand-roll to swap — may already be delegating correctly.

candidates.epoch_check: library available before subsystem epoch (bullmq 2019; crucix epoch 2026-04)
blockers: bullmq requires Redis — adds infrastructure dependency not currently in crucix stack
OSINT sweep-cycle delta semantics (file-based runs/ dir pruning) are domain-specific; bullmq is a job queue, not a general delta store — partial fit only
A6 rated this HIGH severity but the fit is imprecise; leveldb or better-sqlite3 may be closer to the actual need
candidates.verdict_reason: Domain logic (OSINT delta semantics) is non-trivial; bullmq adds Redis infra overhead; better fit may be better-sqlite3 for persistent delta store — needs deeper audit of memory.mjs before committing.

library_status.registry: PyPI (Python) / npm via openai SDK pattern
library_status.current_version: 1.x (Python); no direct Node.js equivalent at same maturity

candidates.epoch_check: litellm is Python-only at scale; crucix is Node.js — language mismatch
blockers: litellm is a Python library; crucix is Node.js — cannot swap directly
Vercel AI SDK (ai on npm) is the Node.js equivalent but is React/RSC-centric
The custom 9-provider factory in crucix includes Ollama local inference — few Node.js abstraction layers handle all 9 providers
This is the most speculative candidate in A6 — factory is 9 lines of provider-select, real cost is per-provider glue code which is domain-appropriate
candidates.verdict_reason: No mature Node.js library covers all 9 providers including Ollama with equivalent maturity to litellm; the factory pattern is thin and provider-glue is unavoidable — not a reinvention.

candidates.epoch_check: library available before subsystem epoch (recharts 2016; crucix epoch 2026-04)
blockers: inject.mjs not fully audited — unknown whether it is data transformation (replaceable) or presentation logic (different scope)
SSE broadcasting is the core role; recharts/grafana are visualization layers — category mismatch until file is audited
A6 noted this explicitly as speculative
candidates.verdict_reason: File not audited; proposed libraries (recharts, grafana) address visualization not SSE data injection — category unclear until inject.mjs is read.

candidates.epoch_check: library available before subsystem epoch; socket.io 2010 vs crucix 2026-04
blockers: A6 rated this LOW severity — current SSE implementation is described as simple and correct
Socket.io would require client-side library change (ws vs EventSource API)
ROI is low unless real-time scaling becomes a requirement
candidates.verdict_reason: A6 audit rated LOW severity and current impl is correct; overhead of Socket.io migration exceeds value for a simple broadcast-to-Set pattern.

summary.post_session_1_adjustments: rate-limiter.ts flipped GO → HOLD 2026-05-29 after Session 1 callsite read revealed variable-per-tenant-quota mismatch.
